intro: Quechua glabrescens, also known as Quechua, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to the Andean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Quechua glabrescens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Quechua glabrescens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
